name: Asymptotic safety (gravity)
summary: Quantum gravity is rendered nonperturbatively renormalizable by a non-trivial UV fixed point of the gravitational coupling under the renormalization group. The theory has finitely many relevant directions, so high-energy predictions are set by a small number of measurements. Predicts specific values for couplings; some scenarios constrain the Higgs mass and quartic at the Planck scale.

addresses_puzzles.note: If gravity flows to a UV fixed point, the Higgs mass-parameter sensitivity to the UV cutoff is reorganized; the tuning is predicted to be modest if the fixed point lies in a particular regime. Highly model-dependent.
ASG predicts the dimensionless Lambda * G_N to flow toward a fixed-point value at the UV; reproducing observed Lambda requires specific irrelevant-direction structure.
The spectral dimension at the UV fixed point drops to d_s = 2 (Lauscher-Reuter 2005). This is a direct prediction of the asymptotic-safety scenario for quantum gravity, not put in by hand.
Wetterich (2018) argued that alpha at low energy is computable from the UV fixed point of gauge + gravity; the predicted value is within ~10% of 1/137 in their truncation.

introduces.note: Number and identity of relevant directions; predictions follow once these are fixed by data.

history: Weinberg (1979) conjectured asymptotic safety. Reuter et al. developed the functional renormalization group framework (1998+); active research into truncation-independent fixed-point existence and matter coupling continues.

references.citation: Weinberg, 'Ultraviolet divergences in quantum theories of gravitation', in General Relativity: An Einstein Centenary Survey (1979)
Reuter, 'Nonperturbative evolution equation for quantum gravity', Phys. Rev. D 57, 971 (1998)
